plan_metadata.name: Simple Bot On V5 And V6 ATK ts playground

steps.description: Click the "Explorer" icon in the left Activity Bar of Visual Studio Code to open the file explorer panel.
Click the "Search Extensions in Marketplace" input box at the top of the Extensions sidebar in Visual Studio Code.
Type text 'microsft 365 agents toolkit' into the search input field within the "EXTENSIONS: MARKETPLACE" panel in Visual Studio Code to perform an extension search.
Click on the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension entry in the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace panel to select and view its details.
Click the "Switch to Pre-Release Version" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel of the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace.
Click the "Install Specific Version..." option in the dropdown menu for the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace interface.

Click the "Select Version to Install" dropdown in the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace, targeting the top entry (version 6.4.3) within the version selection list for the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension.
Type text '5.14.1' into the Extensions Marketplace search bar at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "5.14.1" version suggestion in the extension search dropdown of Visual Studio Code's Extensions Marketplace, specifically targeting the version entry beneath the search bar.
@assertion The version number 5.14.1 appears under the 'Installation' section on the right side, labeled as 'Version'
Click the "Close" (X) button at the top-right corner of the Visual Studio Code window to exit the application.
Click the "Source Control" icon in the left sidebar of the Visual Studio Code interface to open the Source Control panel.

Click the "Create New App" button in the Teams Toolkit sidebar of Visual Studio Code to start a new app project. The button is located directly under "Build a Declarative Agent" and is visually highlighted in blue.
Click the "Bot" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Teams Toolkit extension of Visual Studio Code, as indicated by the red crosshair at coordinates (283, 169).
Click the "Basic Bot" option in the "Select a capability" dropdown within the "App Features Using a Bot" setup screen in Visual Studio Code's Teams Toolkit extension.
Click the "TypeScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Default folder /home/vscode/TeamsApps" option in the "Workspace Folder" selection dialog within Visual Studio Code to choose the default project root folder.
Click the "Explorer" icon in the left sidebar of Visual Studio Code to display the file and folder list for the current workspace.
Type text 'microsoft 365 agents toolkit' into the Extensions Marketplace search box in Visual Studio Code to search for extensions.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension entry in the Visual Studio Code Extensions: Marketplace panel after searching for "soft 365 agents toolkit".
Click the "More Actions..." button (three dots) on the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension details page in the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace interface.
Click the "Uninstall Specific Version..." option in the dropdown menu next to the "Uninstall" button for the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension in the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace.
Type text '6.4.3' into the "Select Version to Install" dropdown input in the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace, filtering available extension versions for installation.
Click the top search suggestion "6.4.3" in the Extensions Marketplace search bar within the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion It appears "Restart Extensions"

Click the "Restart Extensions" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension details panel within the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace tab. This action will apply updates to the selected extension.
@assertion There is a folder called appPackage
Click the "Explorer" icon in the Visual Studio Code Activity Bar (top of the left sidebar) to open the file explorer view.

Click the "Debug in Test Tool" button in the bottom left status bar of Visual Studio Code. This opens the test tool debugging interface for the VSCUSE_APP_JXYRU workspace. The red crosshair precisely marks the targeted circular debug action icon next to the status indicator.
Use the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, enabling access to commands within the active project workspace.
Type text "debug:select and start debugging" into the VS Code Command Palette input box at the top center of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette to initiate debugging. The crosshair marks the exact command selection within the dropdown interface.
Click the "Debug in Test Tool" option in the VS Code command palette dropdown after searching for "debug".
Click the text input field labeled "Type a message..." in the Microsoft 365 Agents Playground web application’s chat interface to start composing a message.
Type text 'hi' into the chat message input box at the bottom of the Microsoft 365 Agents Playground web interface in Google Chrome.
Press the Enter key to submit the message typed in the chat input box at the bottom of the Microsoft 365 Agents Playground web application interface.
@assertion bot reply message: "Echo: hi" exist.
